steps.title: Use the choices as the shortcut
steps.content: The question asks which listed number could be the number sold, so testing choices is faster than solving the whole quadratic. For $n$ products sold, the price is $120-2.50n$ and the revenue is $n(120-2.50n)$.

steps.title: Test the correct answer
steps.content:
For the correct answer, $n=23$. The price is $120-2.50(23)=62.50$. The revenue is:
$$23(62.50)=1{,}437.50$$
That exactly matches the required revenue.

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• A: 23 gives exactly $1{,}437.50$, so it works. ✓
  • B: 24 gives price $60$ and revenue $24(60)=1{,}440$, too high. ✗
  • C: 527 and D: 1,440 would make the price negative, so they are impossible. ✗
A is correct.
